alternatives.name: Credentialing consultants
weaknesses: Expensive for new therapists with no revenue yet
Still requires you to gather all documents yourself
Doesn't teach you the process for future re-credentialing

alternatives.name: YouTube tutorials
weaknesses: Generic, not specific to your state or specialty
No way to ask follow-up questions

alternatives.name: State therapist association resources
weaknesses: PDFs that haven't been updated since 2019
No step-by-step process, just general information
Assumes you know the basic terminology

market_gap_summary: No resource exists that provides a specific, current, step-by-step credentialing process for therapists that covers CAQH setup, panel selection strategy, application timing, and follow-up protocols
switching_reason: A $97 guide that saves one credentialing rejection ($8,000 loss) pays for itself 80x over
